mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or forward returns during rate cuts are statistically indistinguishable from baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained unemployment rate above 9.0 percent signals severe labor market slack that suppresses consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when unemployment exceeds 10%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad-based consumer demand recovery, which boosts corporate revenues and generates positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce positive returns in over 60% of cases, or the interaction term shows no statistical significance in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which directly translates into higher corporate profits and positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain negative or flat during industrial production expansions, or the signal reverses direction when capacity utilization exceeds 85%.

mechanism_narrative: High consumer price index levels combined with stagnant federal funds rates indicate unanchored inflation expectations, prompting market repricing of equity valuations and negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally despite high inflation and low rates, or the interaction effect vanishes when CPI growth falls below 2% annually.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls demonstrates robust employment expansion, which supports household income and consumer expenditure, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Payroll growth fails to correlate with positive 60-day returns, or the signal breaks down when job creation exceeds 300,000 per month.

mechanism_narrative: Positive quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product confirms economic expansion, which improves corporate earnings forecasts and generates positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity markets decline during positive GDP growth quarters, or the signal loses predictive accuracy when GDP revisions exceed 1%.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production alongside rising unemployment indicates a synchronized supply and labor market contraction, which severely dampens corporate profitability and triggers negative equity returns.
failure_condition: Short-term equity returns remain positive despite synchronized supply and labor weakness, or the joint signal shows no incremental predictive power over single indicators.
